Title: Themes and global themes
Summary: Eight color themes, all theme aware. Bundle a theme with a font into a named global theme.
Body:
## Eight color themes

[Preferences → Color theme](/preferences) gives you eight named themes: Dark, Light, Red, Amber, Green, Cyan, Blue, Magenta. Each one re points the accent palette and re tints the neutral grays toward the same hue. The active theme is captured in a `data-theme` attribute on the document root and resolves through CSS variables, so the change is instant and applies everywhere (including [scrollbars](/help/editor-basics/scrollbars)).

## Global themes

A global theme is a named bundle of color theme + body font + UI scale + letter spacing. The default global theme is built in and cannot be deleted; you can save your current settings as a new one through Preferences and switch between them.

This is useful when you want to reach for "the look I had yesterday" without remembering each individual setting.

## Where it is stored

Global themes live in `data/preferences.json`. They are also cached to `localStorage` so the app paints with the right theme before the server response arrives. Without that cache, you would see a flash of dark theme on every page load before the saved theme applied.
